message: ⚠️ FLAG: Meridian Freight - Driver #38 (Carlos Mendez) - 3 fuel transactions within 45 min at locations 80mi apart. Transaction amounts: $412, $387, $218. Auto-held card pending manual review. Risk score for this driver session: 78/100.

thread_replies.message: Looking into it. Carlos runs the Atlanta-Birmingham route - could be GPS lag on the merchant side. Checking with Pilot Flying J.
Confirmed with merchant - GPS coordinates were incorrect on the POS system at the Anniston, AL location. Legitimate transactions. Releasing hold.

message: Meridian Freight asking for line increase from $185K to $250K again. Mike Davis is a great customer but getting impatient with our turnaround time. He mentioned WEX is courting them. Can we fast-track the review?

thread_replies.message: Let's review their file first. Pull last 90 days of spend patterns and updated financials before we approve anything. Also want to check their FMCSA safety rating given the fleet expansion.
Agreed with Delia. But Rachel - let Mike know we're on it. Give him a timeline. We don't want to lose a growth account over process speed.
On it. Will call Mike today with a 3-day turnaround commitment.

message: 🚨 ALERT: QuickHaul Logistics - Utilization spike to 70.7% (expected: ~40%). Spend velocity 74% above model prediction for fleet size. 12 transactions at unidentified merchant (ID#449281) totaling $8,900 in 5 days. Flagging for IMMEDIATE manual review.

thread_replies.message: This one concerns me. The unknown merchant txns plus the card-to-fleet ratio being off (18 cards for 12 trucks) is a classic pattern. Let's pull everything - KYB docs, bank statements, FMCSA cross-ref. James, what's your read from the calls?

Not great. Raj was evasive yesterday when I asked about updated financials. Said his accountant was on vacation. He's been pushing for line increase since week 3. I gave him until Friday to send bank statements.
If docs don't come by Friday EOD, let's reduce the line to $25K and put a temporary hold on new card issuance. I'd rather be safe here.

message: QuickHaul needs full review. Summary of flags: (1) 70.7% utilization in month 2, (2) $8.9K at unknown merchant, (3) 18 cards for 12 trucks, (4) off-hours transactions, (5) late payment in month 1, (6) owner evasive on docs. Anyone seen the FMCSA data? Their DOT# is 4102847.

thread_replies.message: FMCSA lookup for DOT# 4102847: QuickHaul Logistics Inc. Status: Active. Power units: 8 (not 12 as stated). Drivers: 10. Safety rating: Not rated. Last inspection: None on record. Operating since: Sep 2023.
So they told us 12 trucks but FMCSA shows 8. That's a material misrepresentation on the application. This is escalating to high priority. @James Ortiz do NOT approve any new cards.
Copy. Blocking new card requests now. Already had 6 on hold.

message: 🎉 Greenfield renewing annual contract! QBR went great with Linda Park. Big news: they're acquiring Midwest Carriers (~30 trucks). Could be +$60K MRR for us. Linda wants a dedicated onboarding plan for the acquisition. Also requesting API integration and volume discount conversation.

thread_replies.message: Excellent news. Greenfield is a top-10 account. Let's fast-track the Midwest Carriers onboarding. Delia - can your team do a quick risk review on Midwest Carriers before we bring them on?
Already on it. Pulling Midwest Carriers' FMCSA data and will run a standard KYB check. Should have prelim results by Thursday.
Perfect. Also flagging - Linda mentioned their board wants 15% cost reduction across all vendors. We should proactively build a Coast ROI report before renewal negotiation goes further.

message: Feature request from Patriot Plumbing (COAST-6743): They want per-job spending limits that sync with BuildOps job IDs. Currently they're manually reconciling job costs. Dave says this would save them another 5+ hrs/month on top of the 10 hrs they already save with the integration. This could be a big differentiator for field service accounts.

thread_replies.message: Interesting. Adding to the BuildOps integration roadmap. Can James get us a more detailed spec of what Dave envisions? Specific fields, limits, etc.?
Will schedule a call with Dave and the product team next week.

message:
📊 Weekly Activation Report - Low Activation Accounts:
• Apex Solar (COAST-7291): 3/10 cards active (30%). Onboarded 35 days ago. Below 50% activation threshold.
• [2 other accounts omitted]

thread_replies.message: Called Carlos at Apex today. His crew prefers personal cards + expense reports (old habits). Trying to schedule a team training session but he keeps pushing it off.
This is exactly the kind of account we need a better activation playbook for. Small fleets where the owner signs up but the crew doesn't adopt. Ideas welcome.
What if we sent a short video walkthrough directly to each driver? Or an SMS-based onboarding flow? The owner shouldn't have to be the training bottleneck.
